text: Extra CO2 doesn’t just warm the air—it changes our oceans. As CO2 dissolves in seawater, it forms carbonic acid, altering pH levels. This impacts carbonate ions, essential for shell-building creatures like corals and mollusks. But it’s not all grim—marine life is remarkably resilient. Research is ongoing, and scientists are finding ways to help ecosystems adapt. Stay curious and explore how we can protect our oceans. Like and follow for more science insights!
